top_signals.title: Odd Lots: How Franchising Led to the Gig Economy (Podcast)
top_signals.summary: When the fast food industry began booming in the 1950s, it did so via a new business model known as the franchise. This model allowed independent operators to license trademarks from a business like McDonald’s or Dunkin Donuts, and it soon spread across the country, with huge consequences for how Americans are employed. Legal battles fought by franchises eventually opened the door to what’s now known as the gig economy, allowing Uber drivers to be treated in much the same way as the operator of
